hero.title: About Icon Finance
hero.description: We are an independent wealth management and family office advisory firm, built for clients who expect more than a product - and deserve more than a bank.

philosophy.title: What We Believe
philosophy.subtitle: Three principles guide every relationship, every recommendation, and every decision we make on your behalf.

items.title: Client Interests Above All
items.description: We act as fiduciaries - always. Our independence from banks, funds, and product providers means every recommendation we make is driven by your goals alone. Independent in advisory mandates; where transaction fees apply, as in M&A, these are fully disclosed and agreed in advance.

items.title: Long-Term Relationships
items.description: We measure our success in decades, not transactions. Our clients stay with us because we earn that relationship every year - not because they are locked in.

items.title: Confidentiality and Trust
items.description: Your affairs are yours. We treat client information with absolute discretion - as a professional obligation and a personal one.

story.title:
Built Over Time.
Refined by Experience.
story.description: From a small Warsaw practice to an independent multi-disciplinary firm - every chapter of our story has been shaped by client needs.

steps.title: Foundation
steps.result: Independent advice built on practitioner experience, with no conflicts of interest.
activities: Founders with backgrounds in finance and investment advisory

members.name: Oleg Zabolotnyi
members.bio: Founder of Icon Finance with a background in private banking and investment advisory. Oleg built the firm around a single principle: independent advice that serves clients - not institutions.

members.name: Yurii Labenko
members.bio: Yurii leads the firm's operations and client strategy. His experience spans investment management and financial advisory across Central and Eastern European markets.

members.name: Ksenia Romanchuk
members.bio: Ksenia drives the firm's growth and partnership strategy. She brings deep expertise in building institutional-grade client relationships across private wealth markets.

members.name: Maksym Sydorov
members.bio: Maksym develops and manages Icon Finance's partner network - connecting clients with the right specialists across legal, tax, and investment disciplines.

bottomCTA.title: Not Sure Where to Start?
bottomCTA.description: Many of our clients come to us without a clear brief - just a sense that their current situation could be better managed. That is a perfectly good place to begin.

hero.title: Preserving and Growing Capital Across Generations
hero.description: Icon Finance delivers independent wealth management for entrepreneurs, business owners, and families with complex financial lives. We combine institutional expertise with the personal attention your capital deserves.
hero.imageAlt: Infinity symbol - wealth preservation across generations

whoBenefits.sectionTitle:
Built for Those
With More at Stake

items.title: Business Owners
items.description: You have built significant wealth through your business - but your personal capital remains exposed to the same risks. We help you separate, structure, and protect what you have earned, independently of your operating business.

items.title: Entrepreneurs After Exit
items.description: You have sold your company or stepped back from operations and now face a different challenge: preserving and deploying substantial liquidity. We design structures that protect your proceeds and put them to work with discipline.

items.title: Multi-Generational Families
items.description: Family wealth that spans two or three generations requires more than a portfolio. We coordinate investment strategy with governance, succession planning, and family alignment - so capital serves the family, not the other way around.

items.title: International Clients
items.description: Assets spread across multiple jurisdictions demand more than local advice. We bring cross-border expertise to reduce complexity, eliminate redundant tax drag, and give you a single coherent view of your wealth.

philosophy.sectionTitle:
How We Think
About Your Wealth
philosophy.subtitle: We operate without product commissions, bank affiliations, or placement fees. Every recommendation we make is driven by your goals alone - not by distribution agreements or institutional quotas. This independence is the foundation of everything we do.

items.title: Independent Advice
items.description: No conflicts of interest. No affiliated products. We are compensated only by you - which means we work only for you.

items.title: Integrated Approach
items.description: We coordinate investment strategy with tax planning, structuring, and succession - so your financial decisions are always coherent across every dimension.

items.title: Active Risk Management
items.description: Continuous monitoring across all asset classes and custodians. We act on risk before it becomes loss.

items.title: Full Transparency
items.description: Clear, consolidated reporting across all your accounts and holdings. You always know exactly where you stand.

items.label: No product commissions or placement fees - ever
You work directly with experienced professionals, not junior managers
Expertise across multiple jurisdictions and asset classes
Single view of all your wealth, regardless of where assets are held
Strict discretion as a professional and legal obligation
We measure success in decades, not quarters

strategies.name: Conservative Strategy
strategies.description: Focused on capital preservation and stable income through high-quality fixed income instruments and dividend-paying equities. Suitable for clients prioritizing protection over growth.
items: Capital preservation as the primary objective
High-quality bonds and investment grade credit

strategies.name: Balanced Strategy
strategies.description: A diversified approach combining income-generating assets with selective equity exposure. Designed for clients seeking steady growth with controlled downside risk.
items: Equal focus on growth and income

strategies.name: Growth Strategy
strategies.description: Equity-oriented portfolio with meaningful allocation to alternative investments. For clients with longer time horizons and higher tolerance for short-term volatility.
items: Private markets and real asset exposure
Higher return potential over 5-10 year horizon

strategies.name: Opportunistic Strategy
strategies.description: High-conviction, concentrated exposure to high-growth opportunities including private equity, venture, and emerging markets. Suitable for a portion of a larger diversified portfolio.
items: Private equity, venture, and special situations
Maximum return potential with commensurate risk

portfolio.title: How We Work With You
portfolio.description: A structured process built around your family, your assets, and your jurisdictions.

faq.question: What is the minimum investment threshold?
faq.answer: We work with clients at different stages of wealth. For Wealth Management, our typical entry point is $50,000,$500,000 in investable assets, making professional portfolio advisory accessible to entrepreneurs and growing families. For Family Office engagements, the minimum starts from $200,000,$2,000,000, reflecting the broader scope of services involved.

faq.question: How are your fees structured?
faq.answer: We operate on a transparent, fee-based model. Fees are agreed in advance and based on assets under advisory - we receive no commissions, rebates, or payments from product providers.

faq.question: Can I access my funds at any time?
faq.answer: Liquid positions are fully accessible at all times. Allocations to illiquid strategies (private equity, hedge funds, structured products) are subject to underlying fund redemption terms, which are disclosed before commitment. We can also advise on portfolio reinvestment to support the development of your business.

faq.question: How do you protect client confidentiality?
faq.answer: Confidentiality is both a professional obligation and a legal one. Client information is never shared with third parties without explicit consent. NDA arrangements are available on request.

faq.question: How are client assets held?
faq.answer: Client assets are managed within a custody structure designed to protect client ownership interests. The applicable custody arrangement, including the identity of any custodial or holding entities and relevant access conditions, is set out in the client agreement provided at onboarding. Certain asset classes may be subject to fund-level liquidity terms.

cta.title: Schedule a Confidential Introduction
cta.description: Arrange a private consultation with our team. We will listen to your situation first - and only propose a path forward if we are confident we can add meaningful value.

hero.title: When Wealth Becomes a Family Responsibility
hero.description: Icon Finance designs and operates family office structures for families navigating the complexity of multi-generational wealth - from investment coordination to succession, governance, and legacy planning.

whoBenefits.sectionTitle:
Built for Families
Thinking in Generations

items.title: Families with $2M+ in investable assets
items.description: Your family wealth has grown to a scale where informal management no longer works. You need a structured approach that coordinates investments, legal entities, taxes, and succession in one place.

items.title: Business Owners Planning an Exit
items.description: You are preparing to sell your business and thinking carefully about what happens next. A family office structure gives your post-exit capital the governance and management framework it deserves.

items.title: Multi-Generational Families
items.description: Your family spans two or three generations with different financial needs, risk appetites, and goals. We create the structure and processes that keep capital - and family - aligned.

items.title: Families Relocating Internationally
items.description: Moving across jurisdictions brings new tax obligations, regulatory requirements, and asset management challenges. We coordinate every dimension of your financial relocation from a single point.

philosophy.sectionTitle:
How We Build
Your Family Office
philosophy.subtitle: A family office is not a product - it is an infrastructure. We design it around your family's specific composition, values, assets, and long-term intentions. No templates. No off-the-shelf solutions.

items.title: Custom Design
items.description: Every family office we build starts with a blank page. We map your family's full picture before recommending any structure.

items.title: Full Coordination
items.description: Investments, legal, tax, succession, and reporting - all coordinated from one place, eliminating gaps between advisors.

items.title: Governance First
items.description: We establish clear decision-making processes, family councils, and investment committees - so wealth doesn't create conflict.

items.title: Succession Ready
items.description: Every structure we build is designed with the next generation in mind - legally, financially, and in terms of family readiness.

results.title: What a Proper Family Office Delivers

items.label: One place for all family financial decisions and reporting
Governance structures that prevent wealth from dividing families
Coordination across jurisdictions, entities, and currencies
Full discretion - your family's affairs stay within your family
Built to serve not just you, but your children and grandchildren
Investments, tax, legal, and succession under one framework

strategies.name: Single Family Office
strategies.description: A fully dedicated structure built exclusively for your family. Complete control, maximum confidentiality, and bespoke infrastructure - designed for families with $2,000,000+ in assets.
items: Exclusive infrastructure for one family only
Full control over investment and governance decisions

strategies.name: Shared Family Office
strategies.description: Access to institutional-grade family office services shared across a select group of families. The benefits of a full family office at a fraction of the standalone cost.
items: Shared operations, separate reporting and assets

strategies.name: Family Office Advisory
strategies.description: You already have a family office but need an experienced outside perspective. We serve as your strategic advisor: reviewing structures, optimizing processes, and filling expertise gaps.
items: Independent review of existing family office
On-demand senior expertise without full retainer

strategies.name: Virtual Family Office
strategies.description: For families who want family office-level coordination without a physical infrastructure. We act as your outsourced family office, delivering all services without the overhead.
items: Full coordination without physical office costs
Scalable - grows with your family's needs
Ideal for families in transition or relocation

portfolio.title: How We Build Your Family Office
portfolio.description: A structured engagement from discovery to full operational family office.

faq.question: What is the minimum asset level for a family office?
faq.answer: A dedicated Single Family Office is designed for families with $2,000,000+ in investable assets. Our Shared Family Office model is accessible from $200,000, sharing operational infrastructure across several families. Our Virtual Family Office has no strict minimum and is designed for clients with established financial complexity, multiple advisors, asset classes, or jurisdictions to coordinate.

faq.question: How is a family office different from a private bank?
faq.answer: A private bank sells products and manages your portfolio within their platform. A family office coordinates every dimension of your financial life - across all banks, advisors, and jurisdictions - with no product to sell and no conflicts of interest.

faq.question: Can you work alongside our existing advisors?
faq.answer: Yes. We often work as a coordinating layer above existing legal, tax, and investment advisors - ensuring they work coherently together rather than in isolated silos.

faq.question: How do you handle confidentiality across multiple family members?
faq.answer: We establish clear information boundaries at the outset. Each family member's personal financial information is protected, while consolidated family reporting is shared only with designated principals.

cta.title: Schedule a Confidential Introduction
cta.description: Arrange a private consultation with our team. We will listen to your situation first - and only propose a path forward if we are confident we can add meaningful value.

hero.title: Structure That Protects. Planning That Preserves.
hero.description: Icon Finance provides international wealth structuring and tax planning for entrepreneurs, families, and investors with assets across multiple jurisdictions. We turn complexity into clarity.

whoBenefits.sectionTitle:
Built for Those Facing
Complex Tax Realities

items.title: International Asset Holders
items.description: You hold assets in multiple countries - real estate, companies, investments, bank accounts. Without proper structuring, you face overlapping tax obligations, regulatory exposure, and unnecessary costs.

items.title: Entrepreneurs Before and After Exit
items.description: The tax consequences of selling a business can be significant - or minimized with the right structure in place beforehand. We help you plan ahead and protect what you've built.

items.title: Families Planning Succession
items.description: Passing wealth to the next generation across borders involves inheritance taxes, forced heirship rules, and jurisdictional conflicts. We design structures that make succession orderly and efficient.

items.title: Relocating Individuals and Families
items.description: Changing your tax residency or domicile has profound implications for your global assets. We coordinate the full picture - before, during, and after your move.

philosophy.sectionTitle:
Structuring Built Around
Your Life, Not a Template
philosophy.subtitle: Effective structuring is not about aggressive tax avoidance - it is about ensuring that your wealth is held in the most logical, efficient, and legally sound way possible. We work within the law, across jurisdictions, with your long-term interests as the only guide.

items.title: Jurisdiction Analysis
items.description: We assess every jurisdiction where you have assets, income, or residency - and identify the optimal holding structure for each situation.

items.title: Integrated Planning
items.description: Tax planning does not happen in isolation. We coordinate with your investment strategy, succession plan, and family office to ensure full coherence.

items.title: Legal Compliance
items.description: Every structure we design is fully compliant with applicable law. We work with qualified local counsel in each jurisdiction.

items.title: Long-Term View
items.description: We design for durability - structures that hold up as laws change, families grow, and circumstances evolve.

items.label: Eliminate unnecessary tax drag across all jurisdictions
Assets shielded from undue claims, creditors, and exposure
Fully legal structures that withstand regulatory scrutiny
Wealth transfers smoothly to the next generation
All structures coordinated under one coherent framework
Built to evolve as your life, laws, and assets change

strategies.name: Wealth Structuring
strategies.description: We design holding structures for your global assets - companies, real estate, investments, and liquid wealth - to ensure efficient ownership, protection, and management across jurisdictions.

strategies.name: Tax Planning
strategies.description: Trusts and private foundations are powerful tools for asset protection, succession planning, and long-term family wealth structuring. We advise on structure, jurisdiction, and administration.

strategies.name: Trusts & Foundations
strategies.description: Trusts and private foundations are powerful tools for asset protection, succession planning, and long-term family wealth structuring. We advise on structure, jurisdiction, and administration.

strategies.name: Estate Planning
strategies.description: Comprehensive planning for the transfer of wealth across generations - minimizing inheritance tax, avoiding probate, and ensuring your assets reach the right people at the right time.

portfolio.title: How We Structure Your Wealth
portfolio.description: A methodical process from analysis to implementation and ongoing compliance.

faq.question: Is international tax planning legal?
faq.answer: Yes. We design structures that are fully compliant with applicable law in every jurisdiction. Our approach is based on legitimate tax planning - not aggressive avoidance or evasion.

faq.question: Which jurisdictions do you work with?
faq.answer: Poland, UAE, Cyprus, Malta, Luxembourg, and others, always selecting the most appropriate jurisdiction for your specific situation.

faq.question: Do I need to change my residency?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. Residency planning is one tool among many. We assess whether a residency change would benefit your overall position and only recommend it when it makes genuine sense.

faq.question: How do you handle CRS and FATCA reporting?
faq.answer: We design structures with full transparency in mind. All our solutions are compatible with CRS and FATCA requirements, and we coordinate reporting obligations across jurisdictions.

cta.title: Schedule a Confidential Introduction
cta.description: Arrange a private consultation with our team. We will listen to your situation first - and only propose a path forward if we are confident we can add meaningful value.

hero.title: From Growth to Exit - Strategic Advice at Every Stage
hero.description: Icon Finance provides independent M&A advisory for business owners and entrepreneurs navigating acquisitions, sales, mergers, and capital raising. We protect your interests from first conversation to final close.

whoBenefits.sectionTitle:
Built for Owners Making
High-Stakes Decisions

items.title: Business Owners Planning an Exit
items.description: You are considering selling your business and want to ensure you receive full value - with the right structure, the right buyers, and the right terms. We manage the entire process on your behalf.

items.title: Entrepreneurs Seeking Acquisitions
items.description: You want to grow through acquisition but need experienced guidance on target identification, valuation, due diligence, and deal structuring. We act as your independent advisor throughout.

items.title: Companies Raising Capital
items.description: You need growth capital - debt, equity, or a combination - and want to approach the right investors with the right story and structure. We prepare, position, and represent you.

items.title: Family Businesses in Transition
items.description: You are navigating a generational transition, partnership restructuring, or partial liquidity event. We bring both financial and family dynamics expertise to these complex situations.

philosophy.subtitle: Most M&A advisors are paid on completion - which creates pressure to close deals rather than protect your interests. We operate differently: our advice is driven by what is right for you, not by transaction fees or banking relationships.

items.title: Owner-Side Only
items.description: We represent buyers or sellers - never both sides of the same transaction. Your interests are always our only focus.

items.title: Full Process Management
items.description: From preparation and positioning through negotiation and close - we manage every stage so you can focus on running your business.

items.title: Valuation Discipline
items.description: We provide rigorous, independent valuation analysis - ensuring you never leave value on the table or overpay for an acquisition.

items.title: Network Access
items.description: Access to a curated network of strategic buyers, financial investors, and capital providers across Europe and beyond.

items.label: No banking affiliations or conflicted relationships
Rigorous preparation and positioning maximizes outcome
Strict process control - only the right parties engaged
Tax and legal structure optimized from day one
Access to qualified buyers and investors across Europe
One advisor from strategy through signed documents

strategies.name: Exit Advisory
strategies.description: We manage the full sale process on behalf of business owners - from preparation and valuation through buyer identification, negotiation, and close.

strategies.name: Acquisition Advisory
strategies.description: We support buyers through the full acquisition process - target identification, valuation, due diligence, structuring, and post-merger integration planning.

strategies.name: Capital Raising
strategies.description: We help businesses raise growth capital from the right investors - equity, debt, or hybrid - with the right structure and on the right terms.

strategies.name: Due Diligence
strategies.description: Independent financial, commercial, and legal due diligence - giving you a clear, unbiased picture of any transaction before you commit.

portfolio.title: How We Run Your Transaction
portfolio.description: A disciplined process that maximizes value and minimizes execution risk.

faq.question: How are you compensated on M&A transactions?
faq.answer: M&A advisory is structured around a retainer fee covering ongoing advisory work, and a success fee payable upon transaction completion. The success fee aligns our incentives directly with yours: we are compensated for results, not activity. This structure is fully disclosed and agreed in writing at the outset of each mandate. We receive no undisclosed payments, referral fees, or incentives from any counterparty, investor, or third party involved in your transaction.

faq.question: How long does a typical sale process take?
faq.answer: A well-run sale process typically takes 6-12 months from preparation to close. Complex transactions or difficult market conditions may extend this timeline. We set realistic expectations from day one.

faq.question: Can you maintain confidentiality during the process?
faq.answer: Yes. We manage the entire process under strict confidentiality protocols - including NDAs with all counterparties, controlled information release, and anonymized initial approaches where appropriate.

faq.question: What size of transactions do you work on?
faq.answer: We typically work on transactions ranging from €5M to €200M in enterprise value. For larger or more complex transactions, we engage specialized partners from our network.

cta.title: Schedule a Confidential Introduction
cta.description: Arrange a private consultation with our team. We will listen to your situation first - and only propose a path forward if we are confident we can add meaningful value.

hero.title: Beyond the Conventional. Built Around You.
hero.description: Icon Finance provides bespoke advisory for clients with unique assets, complex situations, and needs that fall outside standard wealth management. When conventional solutions are not enough, we design something better.

items.title: Collectors and Art Holders
items.description: Your wealth includes significant art, collectibles, or other passion assets that require specialist management - valuation, insurance, storage, financing, and eventual disposition - handled with the same rigour as your financial portfolio.

items.title: Real Estate Investors
items.description: You hold substantial real estate across jurisdictions and need coordinated advisory on acquisition structuring, financing, management, and disposal - integrated with your broader wealth strategy.

items.title: Startups and Founders
items.description: You are building or have built a venture-backed company and personal wealth is concentrated in a single equity position. We help you structure pre-exit holdings, plan for the liquidity event, and design what comes after, diversification, reinvestment, and long-term allocation aligned with your post-exit goals.

items.title: ESG and Impact Investors
items.description: You want your investments to generate returns while aligning with your values. We design ESG and impact portfolios that do not compromise on financial discipline.

philosophy.subtitle: Standard wealth management frameworks were not built for clients with art collections, complex real estate portfolios, or founders navigating liquidity events. We start from your specific situation and build from there.

items.title: Asset-Specific Expertise
items.description: We bring specialist knowledge to each asset class, art, real estate, founder advisory, rather than applying a generic framework.

items.title: Full Integration
items.description: Non-standard assets are integrated into your overall wealth strategy - not managed in isolation from your financial portfolio.

items.title: Network of Specialists
items.description: We coordinate with art advisors, real estate experts, legal counsel, and founder advisors as needed.

portfolio.title: How We Approach Special Solutions
portfolio.description: Every engagement starts with understanding your specific situation - not with a pre-built product.

faq.question: Can you manage art collections alongside a financial portfolio?
faq.answer: Yes. We treat art and collectibles as a distinct asset class within your overall wealth - with proper valuation, insurance, and reporting - integrated into your consolidated wealth picture.

faq.question: Can you advise founders before a liquidity event?
faq.answer: Yes. The period before a liquidity event, whether an IPO, strategic sale, or secondary transaction, is the most critical window for wealth planning. We advise founders on pre-exit holding structures, tax positioning, and post-exit capital allocation before terms are finalised. Early engagement typically delivers materially better outcomes than post-close restructuring.

faq.question: Does ESG investing mean lower returns?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. The evidence increasingly shows that well-constructed ESG portfolios can match or outperform conventional ones over the long term. We build ESG portfolios with the same financial rigour as any other.

faq.question: Can you help with real estate in multiple countries?
faq.answer: Yes. We coordinate real estate advisory across jurisdictions - working with local specialists while providing a single point of coordination and consolidated reporting.

scenarios.label: ILLUSTRATIVE SCENARIOS
scenarios.disclaimer: Illustrative scenario based on common client situations. Not representative of any specific engagement.

wm.heading: Client Scenarios
wm.subheading: Typical situations we address through our wealth management practice.

case1.title: Founder with concentrated equity in a public company
case1.situation: The client held 70% of personal wealth in a single listed equity, with significant unrealized capital gains and reluctance to trigger immediate tax events.
case1.approach: We recommended and coordinated the implementation of a structured diversification program over 24 months using collar strategies, scheduled tax-aware sales, and coordinated the build-out of complementary uncorrelated allocations.
case1.outcome: Single-stock concentration reduced from 70% to 25% over two years, downside hedged throughout, capital gains realized in tax-efficient tranches.

case2.title: Family with $15M liquidity from real estate sale
case2.situation: A family received unexpected liquidity following the sale of a long-held commercial property and required immediate guidance on deployment.
case2.approach: We recommended and coordinated the construction of a 60/30/10 portfolio across equities, fixed income, and alternatives, staggered deployment over 12 months to mitigate market timing risk, and integrated municipal income streams.
case2.outcome: Capital fully deployed across institutional-grade strategies, year-one return aligned with the family's stated risk profile, and ongoing monitoring framework established.

case3.title: Entrepreneur with assets in three currency zones
case3.situation: A client's portfolio held EUR, USD, and CHF positions but lacked consistent currency strategy, leading to unintended FX losses over five years.
case3.approach: We mapped natural currency liabilities across residences, education, and family commitments, recommended and coordinated the implementation of strategic hedging at the portfolio level, and coordinated quarterly rebalancing of exposures.
case3.outcome: FX-driven volatility reduced by over 40%, currency exposures now matched to actual lifestyle needs, and treasury function operating systematically rather than reactively.

fo.heading: Client Scenarios
fo.subheading: Examples of family situations our office serves.

case1.title: Shared Family Office setup for a multi-generational family
case1.situation: A multi-generational family with $30,50M in diversified assets sought to consolidate fragmented advisory relationships into a single coordinated structure.
case1.approach: We assessed the family's full financial picture, designed a Shared Family Office framework with consolidated reporting, coordinated investment oversight, and defined governance protocols, implemented within a 4,6 month timeline.
case1.outcome: Family achieved full portfolio visibility, reduced advisory costs through consolidation, and established a governance framework ready to scale.

case2.title: Patriarch preparing transition to next generation
case2.situation: The founder of a private holding wished to step back from active management while ensuring his three adult children inherited responsibly.
case2.approach: We facilitated a structured family governance process, including facilitated family meetings to align values across generations, coordination of the drafting of a family constitution through our legal partners, establishing decision-making principles, and coordination of a multi-generational succession framework, engaging specialist legal counsel where required. A formal ownership transition plan was established with defined governance milestones and an investment education programme for the next generation.
case2.outcome: Formal handover completed over 18 months, all three children active in defined roles, family wealth governance now transcends any single individual.

case3.title: Two cousin branches consolidating operations
case3.situation: Two related families discovered duplicated wealth management costs and wanted to share infrastructure while maintaining strategic independence.
case3.approach: We architected a Shared Family Office model with clear separation of investment decisions, joint back-office services, and a governance protocol for shared decisions.
case3.outcome: Combined operational costs reduced by 40%, each branch retains full investment autonomy, and shared services include reporting, accounting, and compliance.

st.heading: Client Scenarios
st.subheading: Representative situations we structure for our clients.

case1.title: Entrepreneur with operations across multiple jurisdictions
case1.situation: An owner of an international consumer brand operated across multiple jurisdictions through a fragmented holding structure, creating exposure to unnecessary tax leakage, unpredictable dividend flows, and unresolved succession risk.
case1.approach: We designed a consolidated holding structure in a treaty-efficient European jurisdiction, established regional subsidiaries aligned with operational substance, and implemented compliant profit repatriation mechanisms.
case1.outcome: Structural realignment within applicable treaty frameworks delivered a material reduction in the group's effective tax rate. Dividend flows became fully predictable, and a documented ownership transition mechanism was put in place for the next generation.

case2.title: Family establishing discretionary trust structure
case2.situation: A family sought to separate designated assets from the risk profile of active operating businesses, while preserving long-term beneficial access for current and future generations.
case2.approach: We established a discretionary trust in a stable common-law jurisdiction, transferred designated assets under a structured settlement, and implemented protector provisions to retain meaningful family oversight without compromising the trust's legal integrity.
case2.outcome: Designated assets were effectively separated from operational business risk. The family retained influence through defined protector rights.

case3.title: Senior professional relocating between European jurisdictions
case3.situation: A senior professional was considering relocation between two European jurisdictions. The move carried significant tax implications depending on the sequence, structure, and timing of the transition.
case3.approach: We coordinated with licensed local counsel in both jurisdictions, modeled long-term tax outcomes under each scenario, and advised on the alignment of asset structures with the client's new jurisdictional profile.
case3.outcome: The residency transition was completed without compliance issues. Pre-move structuring delivered a significantly more favourable long-term outcome compared to an unadvised default approach.

ma.heading: Client Scenarios
ma.subheading: Transaction situations where we have advised clients.

case1.title: Founder preparing business for sale
case1.situation: The owner of a mid-market services company was approached by potential buyers but had not prepared the business or himself for transaction readiness.
case1.approach: We coordinated valuation analysis, prepared the data room, advised on optimal deal structure, and built a personal post-exit financial plan in parallel.
case1.outcome: Transaction closed at a valuation 18% above initial offer, deal structured for tax efficiency, and post-exit wealth strategy ready on day one of liquidity.

case2.title: Family business pursuing geographic expansion
case2.situation: A successful regional company sought to expand into a new country through acquisition but lacked internal M&A capabilities.
case2.approach: We led target identification, ran due diligence across financial, legal, and operational dimensions, structured the financing, and advised on integration planning.
case2.outcome: Acquisition completed within target budget, integration delivered key synergies in year one, and the family's operational footprint expanded into a strategic new market.

case3.title: Growth-stage company seeking strategic capital
case3.situation: The founders of a growing technology company wanted to raise growth capital while preserving control and finding a partner aligned with long-term vision.
case3.approach: We prepared the financial narrative, identified family-office and patient capital investors aligned with the founders' philosophy, structured the round, and negotiated key terms.
case3.outcome: Capital raised at favorable valuation, control retained by founders, and new investors brought strategic value beyond capital alone.

ss.heading: Client Scenarios
ss.subheading: Specialized situations we address for our clients.

case1.title: Family consolidating international property holdings
case1.situation: A family had accumulated over a dozen properties across four countries through ad-hoc decisions, with no consolidated view, varying ownership structures, and inconsistent yields.
case1.approach: We mapped the entire portfolio, restructured ownership for efficiency, divested underperforming assets, and reallocated proceeds into institutional real estate funds for diversification.
case1.outcome: Portfolio yield improved measurably, ownership structures simplified, and liquidity now exists where previously the assets were operationally heavy.

case3.title: Startup founder managing pre- and post-exit wealth
case3.situation: A founder of a venture-backed technology company held the majority of personal wealth in a single concentrated equity position. With a liquidity event approaching, they sought to build a financial structure that would survive the exit and serve long-term goals beyond the company.
case3.approach: We mapped the founder's full financial picture, vested and unvested equity, secondary liquidity options, and personal liabilities. We designed a pre-exit holding structure, built a diversified investment policy reflecting the founder's risk profile and time horizon, and coordinated tax-efficient distribution of post-exit capital across asset classes.
case3.outcome: Pre-exit structure in place six months before close. Post-exit capital progressively diversified across asset classes, with a transition roadmap agreed within the first 90 days. Founder moved from single-stock concentration to a professionally managed multi-asset portfolio with clear governance and reinvestment policy.

scenariosOverview.heading: How We Address Client Situations
scenariosOverview.subheading: Across our practice areas, we work through recurring patterns of complexity. Explore examples of the situations we structure for clients.

servicesIntro.title: Comprehensive expertise, long-term partnership
servicesIntro.body: Working with us opens access to integrated expertise: capital structuring and protection, portfolio management, M&A transaction support, and international tax planning. We build long-term partnerships, as your business and capital grow, our scope of solutions grows with them. Compensation is agreed upfront, transparent, with no commissions or hidden payments of any kind.

thankYou.title: Thank you for reaching out
thankYou.body: Thank you! Your message has been sent. Our team will get back to you within the hour, Mon,Fri, 10:00,19:00 CET. If you reached out outside business hours, expect to hear from us tomorrow.

items.question: What do you cost?
items.answer: Our fees are agreed upfront and structured around the scope of engagement, not product sales. Wealth Management mandates are typically fee-based on assets under advisory. Family Office and M&A engagements are quoted individually. We are happy to outline the fee structure during our first conversation.

items.question: Which banks and custodians do you work with?
items.answer: We work with regulated European and international banking and brokerage institutions. Specific custodian arrangements are disclosed in the client agreement at onboarding.

items.question: What is your track record?
items.answer: We manage and advise on assets for 100+ family clients across 5 jurisdictions. Specific portfolio outcomes are confidential, but representative client situations are described in our case scenarios.

items.question: I have $300K, is Icon Finance right for me?
items.answer: We work with clients at different stages of wealth. Wealth Management advisory starts from $50,000. If you are unsure whether we are the right fit, let us have a first conversation and find out together.
